subprocess_flow.description: The AI Solution Architect initiates the screening process by reviewing the Initial AI Opportunity Assessment and configuring the AI Fit Assessment Matrix tool. This involves setting the project context, identifying specific client industry constraints, and ensuring all preliminary data from the intake phase is accessible for evaluation.
success_criteria: All prerequisite documents gathered and indexed
subprocess_flow.purpose: To prepare the necessary tools and data for a structured and efficient evaluation of the AI opportunity.
subprocess_flow.scope: Administrative setup and data gathering phase for the screening process.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Incomplete data for assessment leading to delays.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Mandatory checklist for required intake information before starting.

change_management.change_request_process: Notification to Bid Manager for missing data.

subprocess_flow.description: Analyze the proposed use case to determine if Generative AI or Agentic systems are the optimal solution compared to traditional deterministic logic. The architect evaluates the complexity of the problem, the need for probabilistic reasoning, and whether the desired outcomes require the specific capabilities of LLMs or autonomous agents.
success_criteria: Clear distinction between AI and traditional logic requirements documented
Technical fit score assigned based on matrix criteria
subprocess_flow.purpose: To ensure AI technology is only proposed for problems where it provides a distinct advantage over traditional software.
subprocess_flow.scope: Technical logic analysis and solution feasibility.
end_conditions: Technical fit confirmed or flagged as unsuitable

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Misidentifying a deterministic problem as an AI problem.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Comparison against a standardized 'Traditional vs AI' decision tree.

change_management.change_request_process: Architectural Review Board submission if fit is borderline.

subprocess_flow.description: Evaluate the availability, quality, and volume of client data required to support the proposed AI solution. This involves assessing whether the client has the necessary data infrastructure to support grounding (RAG) or fine-tuning, and identifying any significant data engineering hurdles that could impact implementation success.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To verify that the client's data environment can realistically support the proposed AI/Agentic architecture.
subprocess_flow.scope: Data quality and infrastructure readiness review.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Use of standardized data quality assessment templates.

change_management.change_request_process: Update to solution scope if data is insufficient.

subprocess_flow.description: Conduct a deep dive into potential ethical risks, including algorithmic bias, transparency requirements, and compliance with regulations like the EU AI Act or GDPR. This step ensures the solution aligns with internal Ethical AI guidelines and identifies any high-risk scenarios that require specific mitigation strategies.
success_criteria: All potential ethical risks identified and categorized
Compliance with EU AI Act and GDPR verified for the use case
subprocess_flow.purpose: To ensure the proposed AI solution is ethically viable and legally compliant.
subprocess_flow.scope: Ethical, legal, and regulatory impact analysis.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Overlooking subtle biases or regulatory nuances.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Mandatory consultation with the Responsible AI / Legal Lead for high-risk use cases.

governance_requirements: Adherence to internal Ethical AI guidelines

change_management.change_request_process: Legal review request for high-risk flags.

subprocess_flow.description: Input all findings from technical, data, and ethical evaluations into the AI Fit Assessment Matrix tool. The tool synthesizes these inputs to generate a quantitative score and a preliminary 'Go/No-Go' recommendation based on weighted criteria established by the enterprise AI strategy.
success_criteria: Matrix fully populated with evidence-based scores
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide a data-driven, objective basis for the final suitability recommendation.
subprocess_flow.scope: Quantitative scoring and synthesis of assessment data.
triggers: Completion of all individual assessment components
end_conditions: Draft report ready for peer review

step_risks_and_controls.control: Weighted scoring system with clear definitions for each numeric value.

change_management.change_request_process: Matrix weight adjustment via Strategy Committee.

subprocess_flow.description: A Lead AI Architect reviews the draft assessment to ensure objectivity, technical accuracy, and adherence to ethical standards. This peer review acts as a critical control to prevent the overselling of AI capabilities and ensures that the recommendation is defensible and aligned with the firm's risk appetite.
success_criteria: Lead Architect sign-off on the assessment accuracy
All feedback items addressed by the Solution Architect
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide an independent check on the suitability assessment to maintain high quality and ethical integrity.
subprocess_flow.scope: Internal quality assurance and peer review.
triggers: Submission of draft report for review
end_conditions: Assessment validated and approved for finalization

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Overselling AI capabilities or recommending unsuitable use cases.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Mandatory review by a lead AI Architect with veto power.

change_management.change_request_process: Revision cycle within the screening project.

subprocess_flow.description: Incorporate feedback from the peer review and finalize the AI Suitability Report. This document provides the definitive 'Go/No-Go' recommendation, summarizes the technical and ethical findings, and serves as the primary input for the subsequent Solution Architecture & Guardrail Design stage.
success_criteria: Final report delivered to the Bid/RFP Manager
Clear 'Go/No-Go' recommendation stated with supporting evidence
subprocess_flow.purpose: To document the final decision on AI suitability and provide a foundation for the technical proposal.
end_conditions: AI suitability and ethical viability confirmed or rejected

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Inconsistent reporting format leading to confusion.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Use of a standardized AI Suitability Report template.

change_management.change_request_process: Formal revision request if client requirements change.
change_management.versioning_rules: Final PDF with timestamp and version ID
